result.instructions: qproof checks code for quantum-vulnerable cryptography and recommends post-quantum (NIST PQC) migrations. Use scan_path / inventory_crypto to assess a path, list_rules to see detectors, and explain_finding / suggest_hybrid for remediation guidance.

note: notification â€” no response is sent

tools.name: explain_finding
tools.description: Explain a qproof finding and its post-quantum remediation. Provide a ruleId (e.g. 'rsa-keygen') and/or an algorithm (e.g. 'RSA', 'ECDSA').

ruleId.description: The finding's rule id, matching a detector id prefix.

algorithm.description: The classical algorithm family involved (e.g. RSA, ECDH, ECDSA).

tools.name: inventory_crypto
tools.description: Produce a post-quantum readiness inventory for a path: a 0-100 readiness score plus counts of cryptographic findings by algorithm, category, and severity.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to a file or directory to inventory.

tools.name: list_rules
tools.description: List the qproof detector catalog: every detector id and what it looks for.

tools.name: scan_path
tools.description: Scan a file or directory for classical (quantum-vulnerable) asymmetric cryptography using qproof. Returns a readiness summary and findings.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to a file or directory to scan.

format.description: Output format: 'summary' (default) for readable text, 'json' for the raw ScanResult.

tools.name: suggest_hybrid
tools.description: Recommend a post-quantum / hybrid migration. Provide an 'algorithm' (e.g. RSA, ECDH, ECDSA) or free-text 'context' describing the usage.

algorithm.description: Classical algorithm family to migrate away from.

context.description: Free-text description of the cryptographic usage (used when no algorithm is given).

content.text:
Migration guidance for: RSA
Detected family: RSA
Recommended replacement: ML-KEM-768 for key establishment (hybrid X25519MLKEM768).
For signatures use ML-DSA-65 (Dilithium) or SLH-DSA (SPHINCS+) where statelessness matters.

Hybrid migrations combine a classical primitive with a NIST PQC algorithm so security holds if either survives. Roll out hybrids first, then drop the classical half once the PQC side is proven in your environment.

Rule: rsa-keygen
Detector: rsa â€” detects RSA key generation and usage.

Algorithm: RSA
Why it matters: RSA relies on hardness assumptions (integer factorization / discrete log) that Shor's algorithm breaks on a cryptographically-relevant quantum computer.
Recommendation: ML-KEM-768 (hybrid X25519MLKEM768)
Detail: Replace RSA key transport/encryption with ML-KEM; deploy as a hybrid during transition.
